title: quercus petraea subsp. mas
intro: Quercus petraea subsp. mas, also known as sessile oak, is a subspecies of the pedunculate oak tree, primarily found in Europe and Western Asia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus petraea subsp. mas include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various conditions, such as digestive issues and skin problems.
